* * *

        extends [Object](<https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")
        implements [Sink](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/Sink.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2")<IN>, [SupportsWriterState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2")<IN,[FileWriterBucketState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/writer/FileWriterBucketState.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink.writer")>, [SupportsCommitter](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsCommitter.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2")<[FileSinkCommittable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSinkCommittable.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink")>, [SupportsWriterState.WithCompatibleState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.WithCompatibleState.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2"), [SupportsPreCommitTopology](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/connector/sink2/SupportsPreCommitTopology.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.connector.sink2")<[FileSinkCommittable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSinkCommittable.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink"),[FileSinkCommittable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSinkCommittable.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink")>, [SupportsConcurrentExecutionAttempts](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/SupportsConcurrentExecutionAttempts.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common")

A unified sink that emits its input elements to [`FileSystem`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/fs/FileSystem.html> "class in org.apache.flink.core.fs") files within buckets. This sink achieves exactly-once semantics for both `BATCH` and `STREAMING`.

When creating the sink a `basePath` must be specified. The base directory contains one directory for every bucket. The bucket directories themselves contain several part files, with at least one for each parallel subtask of the sink which is writing data to that bucket. These part files contain the actual output data.

The sink uses a [`BucketAssigner`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/filesystem/BucketAssigner.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.filesystem") to determine in which bucket directory each element should be written to inside the base directory. The `BucketAssigner` can, for example, roll on every checkpoint or use time or a property of the element to determine the bucket directory. The default `BucketAssigner` is a [`DateTimeBucketAssigner`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/filesystem/bucketassigners/DateTimeBucketAssigner.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.filesystem.bucketassigners") which will create one new bucket every hour. You can specify a custom `BucketAssigner` using the `setBucketAssigner(bucketAssigner)` method, after calling [`forRowFormat(Path, Encoder)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSink.html#forRowFormat-org.apache.flink.core.fs.Path-org.apache.flink.api.common.serialization.Encoder->) or [`forBulkFormat(Path, BulkWriter.Factory)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSink.html#forBulkFormat-org.apache.flink.core.fs.Path-org.apache.flink.api.common.serialization.BulkWriter.Factory->).

The names of the part files could be defined using [`OutputFileConfig`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/filesystem/OutputFileConfig.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.filesystem"). This configuration contains a part prefix and a part suffix that will be used with a random uid assigned to each subtask of the sink and a rolling counter to determine the file names. For example with a prefix "prefix" and a suffix ".ext", a file named `"prefix-81fc4980-a6af-41c8-9937-9939408a734b-17.ext"` contains the data from subtask with uid `81fc4980-a6af-41c8-9937-9939408a734b` of the sink and is the `17th` part-file created by that subtask.

Part files roll based on the user-specified [`RollingPolicy`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/filesystem/RollingPolicy.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.filesystem"). By default, a [`DefaultRollingPolicy`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/filesystem/rollingpolicies/DefaultRollingPolicy.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.filesystem.rollingpolicies") is used for row-encoded sink output; a [`OnCheckpointRollingPolicy`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/filesystem/rollingpolicies/OnCheckpointRollingPolicy.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.filesystem.rollingpolicies") is used for bulk-encoded sink output.

In some scenarios, the open buckets are required to change based on time. In these cases, the user can specify a `bucketCheckInterval` (by default 1m) and the sink will check periodically and roll the part file if the specified rolling policy says so.

Part files can be in one of three states: `in-progress`, `pending` or `finished`. The reason for this is how the sink works to provide exactly-once semantics and fault-tolerance. The part file that is currently being written to is `in-progress`. Once a part file is closed for writing it becomes `pending`. When a checkpoint is successful (for `STREAMING`) or at the end of the job (for `BATCH`) the currently pending files will be moved to `finished`.

For `STREAMING` in order to guarantee exactly-once semantics in case of a failure, the sink should roll back to the state it had when that last successful checkpoint occurred. To this end, when restoring, the restored files in `pending` state are transferred into the `finished` state while any `in-progress` files are rolled back, so that they do not contain data that arrived after the checkpoint from which we restore.

FileSink also support compacting small files to accelerate the access speed of the resulted files. Compaction could be enabled via `enableCompact`. Once enabled, the compaction could only be disabled via calling `disableCompact` explicitly, otherwise there might be data loss.

      * #### getWriterStateSerializer

            public [SimpleVersionedSerializer](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/io/SimpleVersionedSerializer.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.core.io")<[FileWriterBucketState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/writer/FileWriterBucketState.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink.writer")> getWriterStateSerializer()

Description copied from interface: `[SupportsWriterState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.html#getWriterStateSerializer-->)`

Any stateful sink needs to provide this state serializer and implement [`StatefulSinkWriter.snapshotState(long)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/StatefulSinkWriter.html#snapshotState-long->) properly. The respective state is used in [`SupportsWriterState.restoreWriter(WriterInitContext, Collection)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.html#restoreWriter-org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2.WriterInitContext-java.util.Collection->) on recovery.

Specified by:
    `[getWriterStateSerializer](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.html#getWriterStateSerializer-->)` in interface `[SupportsWriterState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2")<[IN](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSink.html> "type parameter in FileSink"),[FileWriterBucketState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/writer/FileWriterBucketState.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink.writer")>`
Returns:
    the serializer of the writer's state type.

Creates a [`Committer`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/Committer.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2") that permanently makes the previously written data visible through [`Committer.commit(Collection)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/Committer.html#commit-java.util.Collection->).

Specified by:
    `[createCommitter](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsCommitter.html#createCommitter-org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2.CommitterInitContext->)` in interface `[SupportsCommitter](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsCommitter.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2")<[FileSinkCommittable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSinkCommittable.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink")>`
Parameters:
    `context` \- The context information for the committer initialization.
Returns:
    A committer for the two-phase commit protocol.
Throws:
    `[IOException](<https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/io/IOException.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.io")` \- for any failure during creation.

            public [Collection](<https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/util/Collection.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.util")<[String](<https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")> getCompatibleWriterStateNames()

Description copied from interface: `[SupportsWriterState.WithCompatibleState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.WithCompatibleState.html#getCompatibleWriterStateNames-->)`

A collection of state names of sinks from which the state can be restored. For example, the new `FileSink` can resume from the state of an old `StreamingFileSink` as a drop-in replacement when resuming from a checkpoint/savepoint.

Specified by:
    `[getCompatibleWriterStateNames](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.WithCompatibleState.html#getCompatibleWriterStateNames-->)` in interface `[SupportsWriterState.WithCompatibleState](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/SupportsWriterState.WithCompatibleState.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2")`

      * #### addPreCommitTopology

            public [DataStream](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/datastream/DataStream.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.datastream")<[CommittableMessage](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/connector/sink2/CommittableMessage.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.connector.sink2")<[FileSinkCommittable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSinkCommittable.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink")>> addPreCommitTopology([DataStream](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/datastream/DataStream.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.datastream")<[CommittableMessage](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/connector/sink2/CommittableMessage.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.connector.sink2")<[FileSinkCommittable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSinkCommittable.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink")>> committableStream)

Description copied from interface: `[SupportsPreCommitTopology](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/connector/sink2/SupportsPreCommitTopology.html#addPreCommitTopology-org.apache.flink.streaming.api.datastream.DataStream->)`

Intercepts and modifies the committables sent on checkpoint or at end of input. Implementers need to ensure to modify all [`CommittableMessage`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/connector/sink2/CommittableMessage.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.connector.sink2")s appropriately.

Specified by:
    `[addPreCommitTopology](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/connector/sink2/SupportsPreCommitTopology.html#addPreCommitTopology-org.apache.flink.streaming.api.datastream.DataStream->)` in interface `[SupportsPreCommitTopology](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/connector/sink2/SupportsPreCommitTopology.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.connector.sink2")<[FileSinkCommittable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSinkCommittable.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink"),[FileSinkCommittable](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/file/sink/FileSinkCommittable.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.sink")>`
Parameters:
    `committableStream` \- the stream of committables.
Returns:
    the custom topology before [`Committer`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/connector/sink2/Committer.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.sink2").
